seoTitle: Remove the Squarespace Logo From the Browser Tab
metaDescription: Replace the default Squarespace logo in your browser tab with a custom favicon to strengthen your brand identity and look more professional.
intro: The default Squarespace icon in your browser tab can quietly undercut an otherwise polished site. This guide explains how to swap it out for a custom favicon so your brand shows up consistently, even in a crowded row of browser tabs. It's a small change that makes a noticeable difference for small business owners, freelancers, and creative professionals.

steps.heading: Access your Squarespace account settings
steps.body: Navigate to the settings area where favicon and branding options live in your Squarespace dashboard.

steps.heading: Upload your custom favicon
steps.body: Replace the default Squarespace logo by uploading an image that represents your own brand.

steps.heading: Preview the change in the browser tab
steps.body: Check the browser tab to confirm your custom favicon is displaying correctly in place of the Squarespace default.

steps.heading: Choose a favicon that reflects your brand
steps.body: Pick a simple, recognizable image, since favicons appear small and need to be clear even at a reduced size.

steps.heading: Consider the impact on credibility
steps.body: A unique favicon reinforces professionalism and helps your site stand out from other Squarespace-built pages.

faq.question: Why does the browser tab icon matter for my business?
faq.answer: A custom favicon reinforces your brand identity and signals professionalism, whereas the default Squarespace logo can make your site look generic.

faq.question: What kind of image works best as a favicon?
faq.answer: A simple, recognizable logo or icon works best since favicons display at a very small size in the browser tab.

faq.question: Is changing the favicon a paid feature?
faq.answer: Uploading a custom favicon is done through your account's settings and branding options as covered in the tutorial.

faq.question: Will the new favicon show up right away?
faq.answer: You should be able to preview the change directly in the browser tab after uploading, though browser caching can occasionally delay the update.
